commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Wisdom rests in the heart of him that has understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known. Protestants, "but that which is in the midst of fools is made known. "(Haydock) A vessel full of gold makes no noise, while that which contains only a few pieces sounds much. ) (Cornelius a Lap ide) Thus the fool makes a parade of all that he knows. (Calmet) Septuagint, "but in the heart of fools, it is not known. "(Haydock) Aquila and Theodotion have the negation, (Calmet) as well as the Syriac and Arabic. (Kennicott).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
